---
editor: visual
---

```{r}
#| include: false
source("setup.R")
```
:::{.columns}

::: {.column width=45%}
```{r age, echo=FALSE}
ggplot(stupiddf3, aes(x = Age)) +
  geom_histogram(
    binwidth = 3,
    fill = "#8B5CF6",
    color = "white",
    alpha = 0.9
  ) +
  
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(15, 95, by = 10)
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Age Distribution",
    x = "Age",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

```

```{r Gender, echo=FALSE}
# Create summary table with percentages
gender_df <- stupiddf3 %>%
  dplyr::count(Gender) %>%
  mutate(
    percent = n / sum(n),
    label = scales::percent(percent, accuracy = 1)
  )

ggplot(gender_df, aes(x = Gender, y = n, fill = Gender)) +
  
  geom_col(
    width = 0.7,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  
  # Percentage labels
  geom_text(
    aes(label = label),
    vjust = -0.5,
    size = 5,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = "#374151"
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Gender of Dating Website Users",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Respondents"
  ) +
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "#14B8A6",
    "#8B5CF6"
  )) +
  
  # Prevent labels from getting cut off
  expand_limits(y = max(gender_df$n) * 1.15) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      face = "bold",
      size = 20,
      color = "#111827"
    ),
    
    plot.subtitle = element_text(
      size = 12,
      color = "#6B7280",
      margin = margin(b = 15)
    ),
    
    axis.title.y = element_text(
      size = 12,
      color = "#374151"
    ),
    
    axis.text = element_text(
      size = 11,
      color = "#374151"
    ),
    
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(
      color = "#E5E7EB"
    ),
    
    plot.background = element_rect(
      fill = "white",
      color = NA
    ),
    
    panel.background = element_rect(
      fill = "white",
      color = NA
    )
  )

```
:::

::: {.column width=45%}
```{r Income Leve, echo=FALSE}
#create new data frame, replace "NA" values w "No Response"
incomedf <- stupiddf3 %>%
  dplyr::count(Income)
incomedf$Income[is.na(incomedf$Income)] <- "No Response"
#clean + order factor
incomedf <- incomedf %>%
  mutate(
    percent = n / sum(n) * 100,
    
    Income = factor(
      Income,
      levels = c(
        "No Response",
        "Studying",
        "$/ 00-20k",
        "$/ 20-40k",
        "$/ 40-60k",
        "$/ 60-80k",
        "$/ 80-100k",
        "$/100-120k",
        "> $/120k"
      )
    )
  )

ggplot(incomedf, aes(x = Income, y = percent, fill = percent)) +
  
  geom_col(width = 0.72, show.legend = FALSE) +
  
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(percent, 1), "%")),
    hjust = -0.15,
    size = 4.3,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = "gray15"
  ) +
  
  coord_flip() +
  
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "#E9D5FF",
    high = "#8B5CF6"
  ) +
  
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%"),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.12))
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Income Distribution",
    subtitle = "Percentage of users by income category",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Percent of Users"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      face = "bold",
      size = 18,
      hjust = 0.5,
      color = "gray10"
    ),
    
    plot.subtitle = element_text(
      size = 11,
      hjust = 0.5,
      color = "gray40",
      margin = margin(b = 15)
    ),
    
    axis.text.y = element_text(
      size = 11,
      face = "bold",
      color = "gray20"
    ),
    
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      color = "gray35"
    ),
    
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(
      color = "gray88",
      linewidth = 0.4
    ),
    
    plot.background = element_rect(
      fill = "white",
      color = NA
    )
  )
```

```{r Education Level, echo=FALSE}
#create new data frame, replace "NA" values w "No Response"
educdf <- stupiddf3 %>%
  dplyr::count(EducationLevel)
educdf$EducationLevel[is.na(educdf$EducationLevel)] <- "No Response"
#clean + order factor
educdf <- educdf %>%
  mutate(
    percent = n / sum(n) * 100,
    
    EducationLevel = factor(
      EducationLevel,
      levels = c(
        "No Response",
        "Diploma Level",
        "High School",
        "Bachelor Degree",
        "Masters Degree",
        "Doctorate",
        "College Degree"
      )
    )
  )

ggplot(educdf, aes(x = EducationLevel, y = percent, fill = percent)) +
  
  geom_col(width = 0.72, show.legend = FALSE) +
  
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(percent, 1), "%")),
    hjust = -0.15,
    size = 4.3,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = "gray15"
  ) +
  
  coord_flip() +
  
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "#CCFBF1",
    high = "#14B8A6"
  ) +
  
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%"),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.12))
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Education Level Distribution",
    subtitle = "Percentage of users by education level",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Percent of Users"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      face = "bold",
      size = 18,
      hjust = 0.5,
      color = "gray10"
    ),
    
    plot.subtitle = element_text(
      size = 11,
      hjust = 0.5,
      color = "gray40",
      margin = margin(b = 15)
    ),
    
    axis.text.y = element_text(
      size = 11,
      face = "bold",
      color = "gray20"
    ),
    
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      color = "gray35"
    ),
    
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(
      color = "gray88",
      linewidth = 0.4
    ),
    
    plot.background = element_rect(
      fill = "white",
      color = NA
    )
  )
```
:::

:::
